mandates.mandate: You are the Symphony Workflow Engine. Follow these instructions EXACTLY.
Execute steps in strict numerical order. NEVER skip or reorder.
Write a checkpoint after every completed step or turn, before advancing.
Quality gates HALT on failure. YOLO never bypasses quality gates, self-critique escalations, or HALTs.
JIT-load skills per step. Evict before the next step loads its own.
The persona file tells you HOW to speak. The engine tells you WHAT to do. You control step execution, not the persona's activation menu.
Stay within 40K tokens per activation via JIT skill eviction.
Every load-bearing failure writes a checkpoint with HALT status before exiting. No silent failures.

inputs.input: Absolute path to the workflow directory (containing workflow.yaml)
One of normal|YOLO|planning. Overrides workflow.yaml default if set.
Absolute path to the run's checkpoint file

execution-modes.mode: Single owner agent, linear step execution, JIT skill eviction between steps
Multi-participant turn loop with persona hot-swap and eviction between turns
RESERVED FOR SPEC 4. At v0.0.2-alpha.1, HALT with "parallel-waves not yet implemented; Spec 4 required." See spec section 5.4 for the Kernel B dispatch call convention that Spec 4 must build on.

interaction-modes.mode: Default. Pause at every template-output (show diff, wait for [c]/[y]/[e]) and every ask tag. Never auto-advance past an ask. When in doubt about whether to pause, PAUSE.

Auto-persist template-outputs without pausing. Auto-answer ask tags from context only when the answer is unambiguous; otherwise still pause. YOLO never bypasses quality gates. YOLO never bypasses self-critique escalations. YOLO never bypasses HALTs. User can say "switch to normal mode" at any time to return to normal pacing.

Before any step executes, invoke the planning-gate protocol. The planning-gate presents a structured plan and waits for user approval. After approval, switch to normal or YOLO per user selection, then proceed to step 9.

step.title: Entry from Conductor
step.action: Receive workflow_path, optional interaction_mode, checkpoint_path from the calling Conductor (or from /symphony-resume)

step.title: Load instructions.xml
step.action: Read {workflow_path}/instructions.xml in full — never use offset/limit
Count total steps N (for sequential workflows) or note that instructions contains only topic/setup/synthesis blocks (for ensemble workflows)

step.title: Preflight check
step.on-failure: HALT with status halted_missing_file or halted_unresolved_variable per the failure category reported by preflight-check

step.title: Adopt owner persona

action.if: workflow.yaml owner is 'orchestrator' or owner is absent
owner is a real agent id
owner is a real agent id

step.action: Adopt the persona's voice, style, expertise, and rules for the workflow duration

action.if: owner is a real agent id

step.action: Do NOT execute the persona's own activation menu, greeting, or internal routing. The engine controls step execution, not the persona.

step.title: Initialize or resume checkpoint

branch.if: checkpoint exists and status is one of halted_unresolved_variable, halted_missing_file, halted_gate_failure, halted_retry_exhausted, halted_user_abort, or faulted

step.branch: Enter resume semantics: read current_step_index, interaction_mode, owner_agent, ensemble_turn_pointer, files_touched, status, halt_reason. Invoke checkpoint-resume protocol to validate files_touched sha256. Present the halt context to the user. Resume at current_step_index + 1 (for complete steps) or re-run current_step_index (for faulted / halted mid-step).

branch.if: checkpoint exists and status is complete

step.branch: Archive the prior checkpoint and fall through to new-run initialization.

branch.if: checkpoint does not exist or fell through

step.branch: Write the initial checkpoint atomically with: workflow_id, run_id, execution_mode, interaction_mode, owner_agent, current_step_index=0, status="in_progress", started_at=now, files_touched=[]

step.title: Pre-start gate
step.on-failure: HALT with status halted_gate_failure and the gate name

branch.action: On user approval [a]: set runtime_mode = normal, continue to step 9
On user approval [y]: set runtime_mode = YOLO, continue to step 9
On user abort [x]: HALT with status halted_user_abort

step.title: Execution mode dispatch
step.branch: Execute the sequential step loop (step-9-sequential below)
Execute the ensemble turn loop (step-9-ensemble below)

HALT with message "parallel-waves not yet implemented; Spec 4 is required. The Kernel B dispatch call convention is defined in docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-08-symphony-core-engine-execution-design.md section 5.4. When Spec 4 lands, it will wrap this engine as Kernel B."

for-each.action: JIT-load all skills and knowledge fragments referenced in step i
Execute step i's actions in order: process action, ask, check, template-output, and invoke tags as they appear
In normal mode: pause at every ask tag and wait for user response
In YOLO mode: answer ask tags from context only when the answer is unambiguous; otherwise still pause

action.if: step i contains an action type='subagent-dispatch' tag

for-each.action: Dispatch a subagent per the structured subagent-dispatch payload. Wait for return. Present summary. In normal mode, wait for user [c]ontinue before proceeding.
Persist template-output to the declared file path via atomic write
Show diff, wait for [c]/[y]/[e]. On [y] switch runtime to YOLO for the remainder of the workflow.
Drop step i's skills and knowledge fragments from context. Persona stays; skills churn. This is the 40K budget enforcement mechanism.
Update the checkpoint: current_step_index = i, append files_touched with sha256

step-block.title: Ensemble turn loop
step-block.action: Read ensemble_participants, ensemble_turn_policy, and max_turns from workflow.yaml
Process any setup block from instructions.xml (load reference documents into the transcript)
Seed the shared transcript with the topic block from instructions.xml

while.action: Select next participant per ensemble_turn_policy (round-robin | facilitator-picks | user-picks)
Log a warning, skip this turn, increment turn_count, continue the loop. Do NOT HALT.

Re-prime: "You are now {participant_name}. Your persona file is below. The shared transcript is below. Ignore any voice carried over from prior turns. Produce exactly one contribution as {participant_name} and stop."
Generate one contribution speaking as the participant
Append the contribution to the shared transcript in-context
Persist the transcript snapshot to workflow outputs.primary path via atomic write
EVICT the prior participant's persona from context. This is load-bearing — it mitigates persona bleed and parallels skill eviction in sequential mode.
Increment turn_count. Update checkpoint: ensemble_turn_pointer, files_touched with sha256.
Check termination: user calls it | facilitator declares consensus | checklist.md stopping criterion met | turn_count == max_turns → set terminated = true
Show latest contribution, wait for user input before next turn
Auto-advance to next turn (user can interrupt at any time by saying "switch to normal mode")

step-block.action: Process any synthesis block from instructions.xml (extract decisions into a structured section of the output file)

step.title: Post-complete gate
step.on-failure: HALT with status halted_gate_failure and the gate name

step.title: Report complete
step.action: Report workflow complete with a summary of outputs, files_touched, and any lifecycle next-step suggestions

category.name: missing_file
category.source: Preflight or step action references a file that does not exist
category.response: Report the missing path and expected source. Ask the user to provide the file or cancel. Wait for response. Retry the step after provision.

category.name: unresolved_variable
category.source: variable-resolution cannot resolve a curly-brace reference
category.response: HALT immediately with status halted_unresolved_variable. Report the variable name and expected source. Do not guess or use defaults.

category.name: quality_gate_failure
category.source: gate-enforcer returns FAIL at pre-start or post-complete
category.response: HALT immediately with status halted_gate_failure. Report which gate (pre-start or post-complete), which check, what was evaluated, why it failed. YOLO does not bypass this.

category.name: self_critique_below_threshold
category.source: self-critique protocol returns confidence below the configured threshold
category.response: Retry the step ONCE (the agent re-drafts). If still below threshold, escalate to the user with the confidence score and a summary of what felt wrong. User approves, rejects, or revises. Write checkpoint only after resolution.

category.name: step_action_runtime_failure
category.source: An action fails (shell non-zero exit, API error, assertion failure)

instructions.step: Mark the step faulted in the current checkpoint with the error capture
Dispatch a Debugger subagent with: error text, step file, prior step outputs, workflow.yaml, any relevant logs
The Debugger performs root-cause analysis ONLY. It does NOT fix anything. It returns a diagnosis: what broke, why, suggested fix target.
Hand the diagnosis back to the step's owner (or a replacement) as additional context
Retry ONLY the faulted step — not prior steps, not the whole workflow, not the whole wave
On retry failure: HALT with status halted_retry_exhausted. Write both error captures and the Debugger's diagnosis into the checkpoint.

diagnose-then-fix.rule: Never blind-retry. Blind retry wastes context and usually reproduces the same failure.
Escalate to the user after ONE retry. Parallelism does not change this threshold.
The Debugger agent's persona, methodology, and diagnosis format are defined by Spec 2b's diagnose-then-fix protocol. Spec 2a defines only this dispatch contract.

halt-semantics.rule: Write the current checkpoint with status set to the appropriate halted_* value and halt_reason populated
Flush current-run state into the checkpoint: interaction_mode, owner_agent, current_step_index, ensemble_turn_pointer
Report the HALT reason clearly to the user: what failed, where, what the user can do
Do NOT advance current_step_index. Do NOT run post-complete gates. Do NOT archive the checkpoint.
Exit the engine. The user regains control.
HALT is a control-flow directive, not an exception. There is no stack to unwind — the engine is instructions, not code.

halt-directives.halt: Variable could not be resolved from global.yaml
Required file declared in workflow.yaml does not exist
Pre-start or post-complete gate returned FAIL
User selected abort at the planning gate or during execution

resume-semantics.rule: On invocation with a checkpoint_path that exists and has a halted_* or faulted status, enter resume mode
Invoke checkpoint-resume protocol to validate files_touched sha256 against on-disk content
If any file changed out-of-band: warn the user, offer Proceed / Start fresh / Review
Re-adopt owner persona per step 5 (unless owner is orchestrator)
Show the halt context to the user and confirm the underlying issue is resolved before retrying the failed step or gate
On status == complete: archive the prior checkpoint and start a fresh run

supported-tags.structural: workflow-engine flow step step-block action ask check branch while for-each
supported-tags.deferred: parallel (not supported in Spec 2a; parallel-waves execution mode is Spec 4's Kernel B)

normative-rules.rule: Execution inside a single Kernel A is strictly serial. No step-level parallelism constructs.
Persona adoption provides voice, style, expertise, rules. The engine controls execution.
The engine writes its current state to the checkpoint after every completed step or turn, before advancing.
JIT loading applies to skills and knowledge fragments, evicted before the next step loads its own.
All load-bearing failures write a checkpoint with HALT status before exiting.
YOLO never bypasses quality gates, self-critique escalations, or HALTs.
Kernel A workflows may dispatch subagents from individual action steps via action type='subagent-dispatch'. Default remains inline.
Variable resolution is two-level only: workflow.yaml → global.yaml.
Workflows communicate through their outputs.primary file paths. No separate handoff protocol.
Ensemble workflows must declare execution.max_turns (default 50). The engine enforces this cap as a termination criterion.
When owner is 'orchestrator' or absent, the engine runs engine-neutral and skips persona adoption.
The engine retries a faulted step at most once via diagnose-then-fix. A second failure escalates to the user.
